title: Train a compact monotone-constrained CatBoost specialist on causal pressure features
summary: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling is specifically designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage in high-cardinality categorical features, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling is specifically designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage in high-cardinality categorical features, which fits `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions. Monotone constraints offer a clean way to regularize physically directional features without hand-coding more thresholds.
implementation_hint: Train a small CatBoost specialist using raw categoricals plus a compact numeric set. Constrain only features with strong one-way intuition, such as `TyreLife`, latent wear, service-window pressure, and cannot-finish pressure, while leaving ambiguous context features unconstrained. Blend this specialist with the current unconstrained model rather than replacing it outright.
expected_effect: May improve generalization on sparse driver/race combinations and reduce overfitting from expanding manual feature recipes.
risk: Over-constraining race-context interactions can suppress true reversals in wet or caution-affected regimes; keep constraints narrow.
